summary: Removed the artificial ItemStateContainer wrapper type from the item system and represented item state data directly as ItemStateTable arrays/pointers. The PR updated ItemLogicTable::states and Item::xBC_itemStateContainer to ItemStateTable*, changed many externs from ItemStateContainer symbols to ItemStateTable arrays, removed address-of uses in item logic tables, simplified state lookup in Item_80268E5C, and moved the capsule spawn/init function it_8027CF00 from handwritten asm into C.

key_files.why: Removed struct ItemStateContainer and changed ItemLogicTable::states and Item::xBC_itemStateContainer to ItemStateTable*.
Removed the forward declaration typedef for ItemStateContainer.
Updated common item state table externs to ItemStateTable arrays and changed ItemLogicTable initializers from &it_803Fxxxx to it_803Fxxxx.
Applied the same direct ItemStateTable array typing and initializer changes across character-specific item logic tables.
Changed state lookup from xBC_itemStateContainer->stateTable[msid] to xBC_itemStateContainer[msid].
Added the C implementation of it_8027CF00 and kept capsule state table it_803F5468 as an ItemStateTable array.
Declared extern ItemStateTable it_803F5468[] and void it_8027CF00(Item_GObj*).
Removed the old handwritten assembly version of it_8027CF00.

what_changed: Deleted the ItemStateContainer forward declaration from src/melee/it/forward.h.
Deleted struct ItemStateContainer from src/melee/it/types.h; the removed type only wrapped an unknown-sized ItemStateTable stateTable array.
Changed ItemLogicTable::states from ItemStateContainer* to ItemStateTable*.
Changed Item::xBC_itemStateContainer from ItemStateContainer* to ItemStateTable*.
Replaced common item extern declarations such as extern ItemStateContainer it_803F54D8; with extern ItemStateTable it_803F54D8[]; in src/melee/it/it_803F1418.c.
Replaced character-specific item extern declarations such as extern ItemStateContainer it_803F6788; with extern ItemStateTable it_803F6788[]; in src/melee/it/it_803F3100.c.

Removed address-of operators in ItemLogicTable initializers because array names now decay directly to ItemStateTable*; examples include &it_803F5850 becoming it_803F5850 and &it_803F8320 becoming it_803F8320.
Simplified Item_80268E5C state-table indexing from &item_data->xBC_itemStateContainer->stateTable[msid] to &item_data->xBC_itemStateContainer[msid].
Implemented it_8027CF00 in src/melee/it/items/itcapsule.c, setting capsule item variables x0 and x4 to zero before calling it_8027D148.
Removed the corresponding it_8027CF00 assembly block from asm/melee/it/it_266F.s.
Added capsule declarations to itcapsule.h for it_803F5468[] and it_8027CF00(Item_GObj*).
smart_moves: Eliminated a fake wrapper struct whose only known purpose was to contain an unknown-sized array, making the real data model clearer: item states are contiguous ItemStateTable arrays.
Changed both producer-side declarations and consumer-side fields together, avoiding a half-converted state where table symbols would still require misleading address-of syntax.
Used array declarations, e.g. ItemStateTable it_803F54D8[], for state table symbols so initializers can pass the array name directly as an ItemStateTable*.
Paired the type cleanup with a small asm-to-C migration for it_8027CF00, reducing dead assembly and anchoring the capsule init callback near the capsule item implementation.
The C implementation of it_8027CF00 directly mirrors the removed assembly behavior: clear two capsule item-var words and tail into the existing it_8027D148 path.
decomp_lessons: Do not preserve wrapper structs merely to model an unknown-sized trailing array when the observed use sites treat the symbol as an array base; direct ItemStateTable* typing can be more accurate and cleaner.
When a table field points to an array, table symbols should usually be declared as T symbol[] and assigned as symbol, not declared as a wrapper object and assigned as &symbol.
After removing a wrapper struct, audit all indexing expressions: former container->array[index] accesses often become pointer[index].
Type cleanup can unlock clearer function prototypes and local ownership: it_8027CF00 belongs with the capsule item implementation rather than remaining as an anonymous asm symbol.
Header and forward-declaration cleanup should be part of type removal; otherwise stale typedefs can hide dead abstractions.
naming_conventions: Retained address-based data symbol names such as it_803F5468, it_803F14C4, and it_803F3100 where semantic names were not established.
Kept existing item-system type names ItemStateTable and ItemLogicTable rather than introducing a replacement wrapper for ItemStateContainer.
Used array extern style for state tables: extern ItemStateTable it_803Fxxxx[].
Used item-specific header ownership for capsule symbols: itcapsule.h now declares the capsule state table it_803F5468[] and init callback it_8027CF00(Item_GObj*).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the handwritten asm implementation of it_8027CF00 from asm/melee/it/it_266F.s after adding a matching C implementation in itcapsule.c.
The decompiled it_8027CF00 matches the assembly sequence by loading item_gobj->user_data, zeroing the two capsule item-var fields corresponding to offsets 0xDD4 and 0xDD8, then calling it_8027D148.

The broad initializer edits are type-directed rather than behavioral: replacing &state_container_symbol with state_table_array_symbol preserves the pointer value while matching the new ItemStateTable* field type.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining uses of xBC_itemStateContainer to verify all code treats it as ItemStateTable*.
Search for other wrapper structs that only contain UNK_SIZE_ARRAY fields and may be removable.
Audit item state table extern declarations for consistency with extern ItemStateTable symbol[] style.
Look for other item callback functions still living in asm/melee/it that can be moved into their item-specific C files.
